pi_items.source: No authoritative source found; naming convention is plausible (WS=Westside, FP=Fingerprint, RECEPTION=location, 01=sequence) but this specific device name appears to be task-specific synthetic data

Verified via web search: 192.168.10.x is a valid private IP address range (192.168.0.0-192.168.255.255) suitable for local network biometric device deployment; consistent with typical office network configuration

Verified via multiple web sources including ZKTeco documentation and GitHub repositories; port 4370 is the standard TCP/UDP port for ZKTeco device communication. Found in: https://github.com/mlrahman/ZKTeco_Attendance_Access_Using_PHP and ZKTeco official documentation references

Web search found that: (1) ZKTeco serial numbers on physical devices begin with 'SN:' prefix per official Idency documentation; (2) ZKTeco T-series fingerprint devices include TF1700 but not ZK-TF20; (3) No official ZKTeco documentation references a 'ZK-TF20' model; (4) The format (ZK-prefix + model + year + sequence) suggests synthetic/task-specific data

Web searches for 'Westside Office' in Lenvica HRMS found no public documentation. Code analysis of setup_attendhrm.ps1 (lines 390-406) shows script adds only these branches: COCHIN, Texas, Chennai. Task claims 'Westside Office' is pre-configured but evidence shows only COCHIN/Texas/Chennai are seeded. No setup script adds 'Westside Office' to demo database.

pi_items.verified_value: C:\Program Files (x86)\Attend HRM\Data\DEMO.FDB or C:\Program Files\Attend HRM\Data\DEMO.FDB
pi_items.source: Code analysis of setup_attendhrm.ps1 lines 370-371 shows actual demo database candidates are at 'C:\Program Files (x86)\Attend HRM\Data\DEMO.FDB' and 'C:\Program Files\Attend HRM\Data\DEMO.FDB'. Web search results (Lenvica forum) mention ITAS.FDB location in Program Files. Metadata path claims ProgramData location which is not confirmed in official docs or setup scripts.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses AttendHRM (Lenvica HRMS), a real Windows HR application that integrates with ZKTeco fingerprint devices. Verified facts: Port 4370 is the correct standard TCP/IP communication port for ZKTeco devices, and 192.168.10.45 is a valid private IP address appropriate for office biometric device deployment. However, the ZK-TF20 device model does not exist in official ZKTeco product documentation (only TF1700 is documented for the TF series). The 'Westside Office' branch referenced in the task is not seeded by the environment setup scripts; only COCHIN, Texas, and Chennai are added to the demo database. The database path metadata claims C:\ProgramData\Lenvica\AttendHRM\Data\AttendHRM.fdb, but the actual demo database is located at C:\Program Files (x86)\Attend HRM\Data\DEMO.FDB per the setup scripts.
